# Think College Level

Cambridge (HSPS) Offer Holder | President of Kiwanis Voice Club of Nippon | CEO of ElevateOS | Lumiere Scholar

Howard Chan's profile, awards, projects, research, service, and contact links.

## Pages
- Home: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/
- About: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/about/
- Awards: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/awards/

- Projects: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/portfolio/
- Contact: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/contact/

## About
- I am Chak Hang (Howard) Chan, a Tokyo-based builder and incoming HSPS offer holder at Peterhouse, University of Cambridge. I work across politics, international systems, AI, service, and entrepreneurship, and I care most about how institutions and teams actually move when the constraints are real. My work sits at the intersection of leadership and execution. I have founded and led Kiwanis Voice Club of Nippon and KIST Key Club, and I keep coming back to the same problem: making coordination clearer, handoffs cleaner, and useful work easier to repeat. On the product side, I build systems for education, events, and operational continuity. The pattern is always the same: reduce friction, improve information flow, and make something durable enough for people to actually use in live conditions. My research focuses on cross-cultural communication and cognitive systems, including Lumiere work scheduled for publication in NHSJS, a cognitive aging review, and a semiconductor supply-chain concept paper. Long term, I want to build technology that works in social and international settings, not just in theory.

## Publications
- Hofstede’s Dimensions and Generational Effects on Ambiguous Emoji Semiotics: Cross-Cultural Analysis of Japanese and Chinese Digital Communication: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/emoji-semiotics/
- Unraveling Cognitive Aging: A Comprehensive Narrative Review Integrating Six Decades of the Seattle Longitudinal Study with Contemporary Advances: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/cognitive-aging/
- Semiconductor supply-chain resilience concept paper: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/supply-chain-resilience/

## Volunteer Service
- Salvation Army SKY Family and Child Development Centre: Gardening and pest removal with SEN children, plus day-to-day support work.
- HKSKH Lok Man Kwok Fong Hin Comprehensive Service Centre: Board-game activities with dementia patients and a five-day Tokyo tour plan tailored to participant ability.
- Shirahige Nursing Home, Sumida-ku: Meals, mobility support, and recreational activities with residents.
- Tokyo / Japan Red Cross: Wheelchair sports event leadership and disability-centered volunteer work.

## Skills
- Leadership: Nonprofit Leadership, Organizational Strategy, Youth Leadership Development, Community Outreach, International Service Coordination.
- Product and systems: AI Product Design, EdTech Development, Platform Architecture, Product Strategy, Software Development.
- Research: Academic Research, Cross-Cultural Analysis, Research Skills, Publications, Generational Differences, Cultural Diversity.
- Languages: English, Cantonese, Mandarin, Japanese.
